title: Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers * Veterans - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers * Veterans 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Veterans Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF HOUSING AUTHORITY OF DOUGLAS COUNTY SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vouchers to assist low-income households with rental costs. Once an eligible household is issued a voucher for rental assistance, the agency pays a portion of the household's rent directly to the landlords. Also issues both Family Choice Vouchers and HUD VASH Vouchers for veteran families. Additionally, fields general complaints about the Section 8 program. 1000 W Stanton Street Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Must meet income and asset guidelines Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-4pm, Friday 9am-noon Email (541) 673-6548 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides help to veterans in accessing VA and community services. Services may include short-term case management, onsite primary care, access to medical, mental health, and substance abuse treatment, and access to VA housing programs, including Grant and Per Diem program, HUD/VASH, Veterans Recovery House and Supportive Service for Veteran Families (SSVF) if found eligible. These grants do not provide immediate assistance, are sometimes one-time, and may include case management. Help with finding market rate or subsidized housing or linkage to community housing programs. Assistance with expediting VA Benefit claims and access to employment programs such as Supported Employment, Veterans Reintegration program, and State employment. Access to computers, clothing, and hygiene items is available onsite. 308 SW 1st Avenue Suite 155 Portland, OR 972043432 Eligibility: Veterans and their families who are experiencing homelessness or at risk of becoming homeless. Veterans must be present in the household and must be the one to request services. Some services available for Veterans who are not eligible for full VA Healthcare benefits. Hours: -Business hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 7:30am-4:30pm, Thursday 10am-4:30pm, closed federal holidays -Walk-in hours: Monday/Tuesday/Wednesday/Friday 8am-3pm, Thursday 10am-3pm. No walk-ins after 2:30pm, better to go earlier in the day. Closed federal holidays. (503) 808-1256 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOUSING AUTHORITY OF DOUGLAS COUNTY SECTION 8 HOUSING CHOICE VOUCHERS VA PORTLAND COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER VA COMMUNITY RESOURCE AND REFERRAL CENTER
